mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5%, further increases compress equity valuations by raising the discount rate on future cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of FEDFUNDS changes on 20-day returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in the unemployment rate reflect improving labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between decreases in UNRATE and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is negative over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirm broad-based economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and equity prices.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and PAYEMS rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Median 20-day SPX return after joint positive prints is zero or negative over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strong output growth that translates into higher corporate earnings and supportive equity markets, irrespective of the macroeconomic regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO growth on 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1 million units (annualized) indicate a strong housing market that fuels construction spending and consumer wealth, leading to positive equity returns.
failure_condition: In >60% of instances when HOUST exceeds 1000, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a high federal funds rate environment (above 1.5%), rising payrolls increase labor costs faster than productivity, compressing corporate margins and reducing equity valuations. The effect reverses when rates are low.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 1.5% and PAYEMS rises, the median 20-day SPX return is positive over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in real retail sales signal abrupt consumer spending weakness, leading to short-term negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Rank IC of the two-decline indicator on 5-day returns is greater than -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained GDP growth (quarterly) reflects robust economic expansion that underpins corporate earnings growth, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The correlation between sequential GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is zero or negative over a 10-year walk-forward test.
